text.title: Backlog Grooming and Roadmapping
text.summary: Backlog grooming workflow with Definition of Ready checklist, and roadmapping with confidence levels (90/70/50%).

list.item: Step 1: Read roadmap.md and constitution.md
Step 2: List features by status: in-progress, todo, backlog (P0/P1/P2)
Step 3: Ask user: "What do you want to do?" — review priorities, take feature, add feature, remove feature, finish grooming
Step 4: For selected feature — check document completeness
Step 5: If no spec → run Writing Specifications workflow
Step 6: If spec approved, no design → run Writing Design Documents workflow
Step 7: If design approved, no impl-plan → run Writing Implementation Plans workflow
Step 8: When all artifacts complete → move from backlog/ to todo/

rule.statement: A feature is ready to move from backlog to todo only when all DoR criteria are met; features without approved spec or design must remain in backlog.
rule.rationale: Moving features to todo with unapproved artifacts causes executor agents to start implementation against draft requirements, producing code that must be discarded when the spec or design changes.

list.item: Problem/need is clear and documented
Acceptance criteria defined (Given/When/Then format)
Dependencies identified and unblocked
Feature is small enough for one sprint (or decomposed into multiple features)
spec.md status: Approved
design.md status: Approved
implementation-plan.md status: Approved
TASK_*.md files created and reviewed
No blockers outstanding
When to run: after completing features, when priorities change, weekly/sprint roadmap sync, adding new ideas
Phase 1 (Analyze): count completed features in done/, count backlog; review milestones
Phase 2 (Review Priorities): "Are priorities current?" — update MoSCoW if needed
Phase 3 (Add Features): for each new idea — Socratic dialogue, create backlog/{NN}-{name}/spec.md with problem statement, add to roadmap.md
Phase 4 (Update Roadmap): Now (90%) detailed/committed, Next (70%) planned/flexible, Later (50%) thematic/vision, Done with highlights

rule.statement: Roadmap "Later (50%)" items must not get detailed specs prematurely; they should have only a problem statement until promoted to "Next".
rule.rationale: Agents over-specify "Later" items because spec writing is their strength. Over-specified items for distant features create false precision and waste spec effort when priorities change — which they do for 50% confidence items.

rule.statement: Roadmap confidence levels (90/70/50%) must be explicit; agents over-estimate confidence without this constraint.
rule.rationale: Without explicit confidence levels, all backlog items look equally certain. The 90/70/50% framework forces honest uncertainty quantification and prevents "Now (90%)" items from competing for capacity with speculative "Later (50%)" work.

text.reference: Print feature status table across all lifecycle stages
